title: Alert Storm Detection
dataSources: All monitoring tool alerts ingested into Splunk
spl:
index=alerts sourcetype=*
| timechart span=5m count as alert_count
| where alert_count > 50
| eval storm="Alert storm detected"
description: Correlated alert storms across monitoring tools indicate major incidents. Detection enables rapid escalation and noise reduction.
value: Correlated alert storms across monitoring tools indicate major incidents. Detection enables rapid escalation and noise reduction.
implementation: Ingest alerts from all monitoring tools into a common index. Track alert rate across all sources. Alert when rate exceeds normal baseline by >5× (indicates correlated event). Use ITSI Event Analytics for intelligent grouping.
visualization: Line chart (alert rate across all sources), Single value (current alert rate), Timeline (alert storm events), Table (contributing alerts).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Multi-source alert correlation.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: All monitoring tool alerts ingested into Splunk.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest alerts from all monitoring tools into a common index. Track alert rate across all sources. Alert when rate exceeds normal baseline by >5× (indicates correlated event). Use ITSI Event Analytics for intelligent grouping.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=alerts sourcetype=*
| timechart span=5m count as alert_count
| where alert_count > 50
| eval storm="Alert storm detected"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Alert Storm Detection** — Correlated alert storms across monitoring tools indicate major incidents. Detection enables rapid escalation and noise reduction.

Documented **Data sources**: All monitoring tool alerts ingested into Splunk. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Multi-source alert correlation. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: alerts; **sourcetype**: *. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=alerts, sourcetype=*. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where alert_count > 50` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **storm** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search when you know the service or episode context. Compare with the ITSI Service Analyzer, episode or notable views, and Glass Tables or drill-downs for the same time range.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (alert rate across all sources), Single value (current alert rate), Timeline (alert storm events), Table (contributing alerts).
grandmaExplanation: We bring alerts and checks from the tools we already use so one place can show whether our coverage and hand-offs look healthy.
knownFalsePositives: Episode and score noise from content changes, seasonality, and who is in scope. We check maintenance and drill paths before we escalate.
